body: PR description/linkage says this closes Issue #15 (which requests adding `cube` to calc.sh), but this PR only changes calc.py/test_calc.py. Since calc.sh already contains a `cube` operator, consider updating the PR description (or the issue being closed) so the tracking matches what is actually changed here.
these should not be part of the repo

## Pull request overview Adds a `cube` unary operation to the Python calculator (`calc.py`) and extends the Python test runner to validate `cube`, bringing it closer to feature parity with the shell implementation. **Changes:** - Updated `calc.py` argument parsing to allow unary operations and implemented `cube` (`x^3`). - Updated `calc.py` usage/help text to reflect the new unary form. - Added `cube` test cases to `test_calc.py` (positive, zero, negative). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | calc.py | Adds unary op handling for `cube` and updates CLI usage/docs accordingly | | test_calc.py | Adds test coverage for the new `cube` operation |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **calc.py:11** * The module docstring says division uses “floor division for /”, but the implementation uses truncating division (int(num1 / num2)) to match bash arithmetic semantics. Please update the docstring to reflect truncation (or change the implementation to true floor division and adjust tests/behavior accordingly). ``` Mirrors the behaviour of calc.sh: - All operands and results are integers (floor division for /) - Use 'x' for multiplication (avoids shell glob expansion) - Prints result to stdout; errors go to stderr with exit code 1 """ ```
